_doc: Auditable prose corrections applied to scraped concept markdown in guidance.mjs, AFTER html->markdown conversion. Each patch is a literal find/replace with a rationale. guidance.mjs fails the build if a `find` no longer matches (stale patch = upstream text changed = a human should re-verify).

color.why: canonical package name — verified against upstream packages/web/package.json (@material/material-color-utilities ^0.4.0) at the pinned SHA

color.find: Design tokens are named, platform-agnostic variables that represent visual design decisions—such as color, typography, spacing, and elevation—in a reusable, consistent format.
color.replace: Each token gives one visual decision—a color, type style, spacing step, or elevation level—a stable name, so the same value is defined once and reused consistently across platforms and components.
color.why: D6: the phrase 'named, platform-agnostic variables that represent visual design decisions … in a reusable, consistent format' reads near-verbatim to Google's canonical m3.material.io design-tokens definition. Re-expressed in original words; facts preserved; citation kept on the preceding sentence. m3.material.io is a JS SPA and could not be fetched to diff, so this is a conservative rewrite.

density.find: Design tokens are named, platform-agnostic variables that represent visual design decisions—such as color, typography, spacing, and elevation—in a reusable, consistent format.
density.replace: Each token gives one visual decision—a color, type style, spacing step, or elevation level—a stable name, so the same value is defined once and reused consistently across platforms and components.
density.why: D6: same near-verbatim Google design-tokens definition as on the color page; re-expressed in original words, facts preserved, citation kept. See color patch rationale.

motion.find: have adopted the [Material Design 3 motion physics system](https://m3.material.io/styles/motion/overview/how-it-works), bringing spring-based dynamics and velocity-aware responsiveness to components where expressive motion is essential.
motion.replace: have adopted a spring-based motion physics system — an m3e implementation of Material's [motion physics](https://m3.material.io/styles/motion/overview/how-it-works). Exactly which components use physics-based motion (buttons, loading indicators, switches) is library behavior, not stated by the cited spec page. (m3e behavior)
motion.why: the physics-motion component list is m3e-specific; detach it from the M3 spec citation (keep the link for the general physics system)

motion.find: Design tokens are named, platform-agnostic variables that represent visual design decisions—such as color, typography, spacing, and elevation—in a reusable, consistent format.
motion.replace: Each token gives one visual decision—a color, type style, spacing step, or elevation level—a stable name, so the same value is defined once and reused consistently across platforms and components.
motion.why: D6: same near-verbatim Google design-tokens definition as on the color page; re-expressed in original words, facts preserved, citation kept. See color patch rationale.

typography.why: re-point the bare /styles/typography citation at the canonical /overview subpage

typography.find: The system defines a **30-style type scale**—comprising 15 baseline and 15 emphasized roles.
typography.replace: The system defines the 15 baseline type roles; m3e also surfaces the **emphasized** tier introduced in Material 3 Expressive, for a 30-style scale (15 baseline + 15 emphasized). The emphasized styles and their `--md-sys-typescale-emphasized-*` tokens are an M3 Expressive / m3e convention, not part of the classic baseline type scale. (m3e behavior)
typography.why: the emphasized tier is an M3 Expressive addition originating in matraic's docs; the live SPA spec could not be scraped to confirm the generic /styles/typography page states '30 styles', so attribute the emphasized roles to Expressive/m3e rather than the baseline spec
